title: Applied Python Projects: Object-Oriented Apps
description: Build multifile Python apps with the four OOP pillars: encapsulation, abstraction, inheritance, and polymorphism.
audience: Learners who finished Python fundamentals and data/functions modules.
Beginners ready to build larger multifile Python projects.
goals: Use class files, helper files, service files, tests, data files, and generated report files in one course path.
Practice encapsulation before inheritance, then add polymorphism and abstraction deliberately.
Finish with a cumulative OOP capstone where every step starts from the previous full solution.

notes: Every technical sketch must receive a required embedded try-it code_input with id/exerciseKey formatted as try-<topic-id>-sketchN.
Embedded try-it exercises must carry their full starterFiles and solutionFiles just like larger practice/project exercises.
Do not put generated output files such as reports/summary.txt in requiredFiles; they may appear in solutionFiles or runtime outputs.
Module project and capstone steps are progressive: step N+1 starterFiles must equal step N full solutionFiles.
